name: tradingview_grid1
title: TradingView Supercharts Chart Grid Spec
intent: Builder-agent requirements for recreating only the TradingView Supercharts chart grid surface.

include: volume overlay inside the chart pane
current price dotted line and price marker
empty/loading/error states inside the grid bounds
zoom, pan, hover, latest-price pinning, and resize behavior
exclude: watchlist, details, news, key stats, and side panels
bottom date-range toolbar outside the chart grid
alerts, replay, publish, trade, account, social, and brokerage features

localImplementationContext.currentRenderer: Canvas2D in a Next.js client component
notes: The existing local chart already owns market data, wheel zoom, drag pan, crosshair, right price labels, bottom time labels, candles, MA20, and volume.
This spec is a build contract, not a request to change package renderer architecture.

toolsUsed: CodeGraph MCP for local repo orientation
Playwright MCP for live TradingView DOM measurements and screenshots
browser-use MCP for secondary visual reachability check
Camoufox MCP attempted first but TradingView static bundle resolution failed
MCP-Docker Playwright attempted but the browser executable was not installed in that MCP context

axisSizing.rule: plotPane.width = root.width - rightPriceAxisWidthPx; plotPane.height = root.height - bottomTimeAxisHeightPx

layering.name: background-grid-layer
contents: solid or subtle gradient chart background

layering.name: series-layer
contents: optional moving averages or line/area series if already supported

layoutContract.implementationNote: TradingView uses separate canvases for plot, price axis, time axis, and corner. The builder may use multiple canvases or one canvas with strict clipping, but the visible geometry and interaction behavior must match this contract.

typography.axisFont: 11px ui-monospace or system sans depending local app convention

priceScale.labelFormat: locale number with 2 decimals for BTC-like prices

currentPriceMarker.shape: rectangular tag attached to right axis
lines: countdown or elapsed update time when available

hoverPriceMarker.shape: rectangular tag attached to right axis

hoverTimeMarker.shape: floating rectangular label above time axis

volume.placement: overlayed in lower part of plot pane
volume.legend: Vol · BTC 10.5K style in top-left overlay

currentPriceLine.color: latest candle direction or currentPrice token

legend.maxWidthRule: truncate or wrap before overlapping price axis

rows.name: instrument
rows.content: symbol display name, timeframe, exchange, realtime status dot, OHLC values, absolute and percent change

rows.name: volume
rows.content: Vol · quote asset and latest volume

legend.behavior: When crosshair is active, OHLC and volume values reflect the hovered candle; otherwise they reflect the latest candle.

buySellBadges.note: TradingView shows SELL/BUY quote badges inside the pane, but the requested scope is the grid itself. Do not add them unless the builder is explicitly asked for trade quote badges.

watermark.note: Do not recreate TradingView branding. If the local product needs a watermark, use ProCharting branding in a separate product decision.

eventMarkers.shape: small circular icon anchored near bottom of plot pane

pointer.leaveBehavior: hide crosshair and hover axis labels
hoverBehavior: snap active candle index by x position
show vertical dashed crosshair through plot pane
show horizontal dashed crosshair through plot pane
show hover price marker on right axis
show hover time marker on bottom axis

pan.input: primary pointer drag on plot pane
pan.verticalDrag: optional price-scale pan only if implemented deliberately
pan.bounds: cannot pan beyond first candle or beyond latest candle plus right padding

zoom.pinch: zoom around gesture midpoint on touch devices
zoom.timeAxisDrag: optional horizontal zoom if builder has time

optional: ArrowLeft and ArrowRight move hovered candle when canvas is focused
plus and minus zoom when canvas is focused

mobile.legendMode: compact symbol row with ellipsis for overflow
mobile.plotPaneRule: Do not reduce price axis below 80px; let the plot pane shrink and reduce tick density.

dataContract.timeUnit: milliseconds since Unix epoch unless the existing local API says otherwise
dataContract.handleMissingVolume: render no volume bar or zero-height bar
dataContract.handleNonFiniteValues: skip invalid candles and surface a grid-bounded error state if no valid candles remain

loading.style: subtle overlay; do not resize axes
loading.canvasBehavior: keep prior chart visible if refreshing; show skeleton only for first load

empty.axes: may remain visible with neutral grid

actions: retry if data fetch is owned by the same component
error.style: compact panel, not a marketing card

acceptanceCriteria: The delivered UI contains the chart grid only and does not include TradingView top/left/right/bottom app chrome.
At 1440x900, the plot pane, price axis, time axis, and corner preserve TradingView-like geometry: right axis about 80px and bottom time axis about 28px.
At 430x932 and 360x800, the price axis remains readable at about 80px, the time axis remains about 28px, and the plot pane shrinks without horizontal overflow.
Candles use TradingView-like bull/bear colors and 1px wicks, with bodies clipped inside the plot pane.
Volume appears as an overlay in the lower plot pane, not as a separate outer panel.
Current price line is dotted and has a right-axis marker with price text.
Crosshair hover shows dashed vertical and horizontal lines plus price/time markers and updates the legend values.
Wheel zoom and drag pan remain smooth and do not desynchronize candles, gridlines, price labels, or time labels.
The canvas is nonblank after data loads and redraws cleanly after resize and device-pixel-ratio changes.
No visible text overlaps the price axis, time axis, legend, current price marker, or volume bars.

checks: right price axis is visible and about 80px
bottom time axis is visible and about 28px
no surrounding TradingView chrome was recreated
time labels are reduced and do not collide
legend truncates instead of overlapping axes
crosshair appears only inside plot pane

builderNotes: Prefer simple, direct Canvas2D drawing in the existing local chart component unless the builder is explicitly moving this into the reusable package renderer.
Keep the grid contract independent from data-provider work; the component should accept normalized candles and render them.
Avoid adding new dependencies for basic chart grid drawing.
If local code already has equivalent helpers for price formatting, nice tick intervals, and candle viewport math, reuse them.
Do not update ARCHITECTURE.md for this spec-only task unless the builder later changes runtime architecture.
